subject: Public Health
title: The Opioid Crisis
description: A quick win: understand The Opioid Crisis.

hook.text: Pharma companies marketed opioid painkillers as safe—triggering an epidemic that has killed over 500,000 Americans.

buildup.text: OxyContin was promoted as non-addictive. Doctors prescribed it widely. Patients became dependent.

discovery.text: Fentanyl—50 times stronger than heroin—now dominates the illicit supply, causing most overdose deaths.

twist.text: Purdue Pharma was found guilty of misleading marketing—but the crisis still claims 80,000 US lives per year.

climax.text: Naloxone—an opioid overdose reversal drug—saves lives instantly and is being distributed widely to first responders.

punchline.text: A pharmaceutical company started this crisis. Public health must end it.

quiz.question: What makes fentanyl particularly dangerous?
options: It is 50 times stronger than heroin
